source.multipart_basis: Design-specific decomposition of a SAE Brasil AeroDesign Regular-class airframe expanded around the wing spar focus: wing main spar, ~10 ribs, skin panels, fuselage truss, tail boom, tail surfaces, main landing gear, motor mount, and payload bay. No rulebook citations fabricated; all load magnitudes and pass/fail thresholds preserved from the original brief.

full_prompt:
Design the primary wing spar of a SAE Brasil AeroDesign 2025 Regular class aircraft. Regular class constraints: wingspan b <= 2.5 m, takeoff distance <= 61 m (200 ft), maximum take-off weight (MTOW = empty + payload) optimized to maximize payload score per the SAE Brasil scoring formula. For this design target: wingspan 2.4 m, rectangular planform with chord 0.32 m, NACA 4412 airfoil, empty weight (We) 3.2 kg, payload weight (Wp) 12 kg, giving MTOW 15.2 kg. Cruise speed 16 m/s at sea level ISA (density 1.225 kg/m^3). Takeoff loads drive spar sizing because the full 15.2 kg load is supported at ~ 1.2 g near liftoff with maximum dynamic pressure on the wing. The spar is a rectangular-section hollow box beam running the full span, fabricated from balsa caps + carbon pultruded-rod caps + 1 mm plywood web sandwich, simplified in FEA as a homogeneous transversely isotropic beam with effective E 35 GPa, tensile 400 MPa, compressive 350 MPa, density 650 kg/m^3 (composite average). Spar nominal cross-section 30 mm x 15 mm x 2 mm wall-equivalent. Wing-root joins the fuselage via two M4 steel bolts. Load cases: (LC1) static 1 g trim with elliptical lift distribution across 2.4 m span supporting 15.2 kg (total lift 149 N); (LC2) +2.5 g pull-up at dive speed (limit): total lift 373 N elliptical; (LC3) ultimate 1.5 x LC2 = 3.75 g equivalent, total 559 N - per SAE Brasil structural conservative factor; (LC4) landing hard-impact 3 g on MLG attached to fuselage while wings are in 1 g trim; (LC5) ground gust wingtip 80 N horizontal; (LC6) scoring-weight: SAE Brasil Regular payload scoring is (Wp)^2 / (We) - a design optimization metric; spar must survive the declared MTOW, not scored directly here. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC3 ultimate, max composite stress must remain below 200 MPa (400 / 2.0 FoS) in tension and 175 MPa (350 / 2.0) in compression; (R2) wingtip vertical deflection under LC1 must remain below 2% of half-span (24 mm); (R3) first wing bending natural frequency must exceed 6 Hz to clear servo bandwidth; (R4) spar dry mass must not exceed 0.45 kg to preserve We budget; (R5) root bolt bearing stress under LC3 ultimate must remain below 350 MPa. Thermal, fluid, radiation analyses not required; buckling check captured by compressive stress gate at this slenderness. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of spar, composite cap stack-up (balsa + pultruded rod schedule), FEA for LC1-LC5, modal check, mass report.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
Delivered as a multi-part assembly reflecting the physical AeroDesign airframe integrated around the spar: the CFRP/balsa wing main spar, wing ribs distributed along the span, balsa-sheet skin panels bonded to the rib/spar structure, a balsa+ply fuselage truss housing the payload bay, a tail boom extending aft, tail surfaces (horizontal + vertical stabilizers), main landing gear bonded/bolted under the fuselage, and an engine/motor mount at the firewall. The spar root pins through 2x M4 steel bolts into the fuselage truss. Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC3 ultimate). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the primary wing spar of a SAE Brasil AeroDesign 2025 Regular class aircraft. Regular class constraints: wingspan <= 2.5 m, takeoff distance <= 61 m (200 ft), MTOW optimized to maximize payload score per SAE Brasil scoring formula.

Envelope: wingspan 2.4 m, rectangular planform, chord 0.32 m, NACA 4412 airfoil, empty weight 3.2 kg, payload 12 kg, MTOW 15.2 kg. Cruise 16 m/s at sea level ISA (1.225 kg/m^3). Spar rectangular-section hollow box running full span; nominal cross-section 30 mm x 15 mm x 2 mm wall-equivalent; wing-root joins fuselage via two M4 steel bolts.

Material: balsa caps + carbon pultruded-rod caps + 1 mm plywood web sandwich, simplified as homogeneous transversely isotropic (effective E 35 GPa, tensile 400 MPa, compressive 350 MPa).

Load cases: (LC1) static 1 g trim with elliptical lift on 15.2 kg (total 149 N); (LC2) +2.5 g pull-up at dive speed: total 373 N elliptical; (LC3) ultimate 1.5 x LC2 = 3.75 g, total 559 N per SAE Brasil conservative factor; (LC4) landing 3 g on MLG at fuselage while wings in 1 g trim; (LC5) ground gust wingtip 80 N horizontal.

Pass/fail: (R1) LC3 ultimate max composite stress < 200 MPa tension (400/2.0) and 175 MPa compression (350/2.0); (R2) LC1 wingtip deflection < 24 mm (2% half-span); (R3) first wing bending > 6 Hz; (R4) spar dry mass <= 0.45 kg; (R5) LC3 root bolt bearing < 350 MPa.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: SAE Brasil AeroDesign Regular scoring formula (Wp)^2 / (We) is an outer-loop design optimizer that calls the structural FEA to validate each MTOW candidate.

prompt.title: SAE Brasil AeroDesign 2025 Regular Class Airframe (multi-part assembly around wing spar)
prompt.brief: Balsa + pultruded-CF composite box-beam wing spar + Regular-class airframe, 2.4 m span, 15.2 kg MTOW, +2.5 g limit, ultimate 1.5x, SAE Brasil FoS. Decomposition: wing main spar, ~10 ribs, skin panels, fuselage truss, tail boom, tail surfaces, MLG, motor mount, payload bay. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

material.name: Balsa + pultruded-CFRP caps + plywood web (composite effective)

parts.name: wing_main_spar
parts.role: full-span hollow box-beam wing spar with balsa + pultruded CFRP caps + ply web; primary wing bending member

geometric_constraints.cap_taper: stepped pultruded-rod count from root to tip

parts.material_hint: balsa + pultruded CFRP caps + 1 mm plywood shear web

parts.name: wing_rib
parts.role: NACA 4412 rib, laser-cut balsa, threaded onto spar at regular station intervals along the span

parts.name: skin_panel
parts.role: balsa / Monokote skin panels bonded over ribs forming the wing aerodynamic surface and D-tube torsion box

geometric_constraints.d_tube_wrap: from leading edge to spar on upper + lower (2 pieces per side)

parts.name: fuselage_truss
parts.role: balsa/ply fuselage truss carrying wing root, payload bay, MLG mount, and firewall

parts.name: tail_boom
parts.role: aft-extending boom tying fuselage to empennage

parts.name: tail_surfaces
parts.role: horizontal + vertical stabilizer/elevator/rudder assemblies bolted/bonded to tail boom

parts.name: main_landing_gear
parts.role: glass/CFRP leaf-spring main landing gear bolted to fuselage truss; reacts 3 g hard-landing LC4

parts.name: motor_mount
parts.role: firewall/motor mount at front of fuselage truss carrying the propeller motor

parts.name: payload_bay
parts.role: internal payload compartment within fuselage truss retaining 12 kg payload during LC1-LC4

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: aircraft axes: X along fuselage (nose+), Y along wing (right+), Z up; origin at firewall

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces (spar-rib slots, rib-skin bond, wing-root 2x M4 into fuselage, MLG bolts, motor mount, tail boom) <= 1.0 under LC3 ultimate.
Wing-root-to-fuselage 2x M4 bolted joint (IF4) bearing in ply bulkhead under LC3 ultimate must remain below 35 MPa (plywood bearing allowable).

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.
Generated coverage gate: ensures the declared load case is present in the CCX deck and checked from model.dat.
Generated coverage gate: ensures the declared load case is present in the CCX deck and checked from model.dat.
Generated coverage gate: ensures the declared load case is present in the CCX deck and checked from model.dat.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam with orthotropic composite properties for spar; shell for skins/ribs; TET10 near root bolt region

interfaces.mediated_by: ribs threaded onto spar with CA-glue bond at each station
interfaces.load_path: lift distribution from ribs into spar at ~10 stations; primary LC1/LC3 wing load path

interfaces.mediated_by: skin bonded to rib perimeters forming D-tube torsion box
interfaces.load_path: wing torsion (LC5 ground gust) closed through skin-rib bond

interfaces.mediated_by: skin bonded directly to spar cap forming D-tube leading-edge wrap
interfaces.load_path: closes shear flow in leading-edge torsion box

interfaces.mediated_by: 2x M4 steel bolts through spar root bushings into plywood bulkhead
interfaces.load_path: wing root bending + shear into fuselage; governs R5 bearing gate
LC4 hard-landing 3 g reacted from MLG into fuselage truss
engine thrust + vibration into fuselage truss

interfaces.mediated_by: tail boom bonded into aft fuselage bulkhead
interfaces.load_path: tail-surface aero loads transferred forward into fuselage
horizontal + vertical tail aero loads into boom

interfaces.mediated_by: payload bay retained within fuselage by hook-and-loop + perimeter tape
interfaces.load_path: 12 kg payload inertial reaction into fuselage under LC1-LC4

fem_check_policy.rationale: Tip deflection (R2) and modal (R3) only meaningful on the rib+skin+spar D-tube; root bolt bearing (R5) requires spar, bushing, and plywood bulkhead together; MLG landing (LC4) and tail aero loads are whole-airframe load paths.

notes.why_multi_part: The physical AeroDesign airframe is an assembly of spar, ribs, skins, fuselage truss, tail, MLG, and motor mount; the spar alone cannot satisfy the LC4 MLG landing path or the payload retention requirement.
notes.speculation_caveats: Count of ribs (~10) and skin panel subdivisions are typical for a 2.4 m AeroDesign wing; no new rulebook thresholds invented.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
